response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced polyglot engineer with strong distributed-systems and event-driven architecture chops, but the role demands deep, sustained C++ expertise as its primary technical axis — something virtually absent here. C++ appears only in a six-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project; it is not even listed in the candidate's own skills section. There is no evidence of STL mastery, multi-threaded C++ systems, industrial-protocol familiarity (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), IEC 62443 security standards, or industrial-automation domain knowledge, all of which are central to this role.
strengths: Demonstrated ability to build high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby) shows relevant performance engineering mindset
Event-driven architecture experience (GISCollective geo-data platform, Dunnhumby microservices) aligns with the message-broker and async design patterns the role requires
Strong agile/craftsmanship foundation (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based dev at RIDE) and mentoring track record (workshops, dojos) match the behavioral and team-leadership expectations
concerns: C++ is effectively absent from the candidate's active skill set — the only direct exposure is a 6-month role in 2014–2015 and a university project; the self-reported skills list omits it entirely
No industrial automation, IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards, SDL practices, or industrial-protocol knowledge (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, MQTT), which are core requirements rather than nice-to-haves
Career trajectory is web/TypeScript/Node.js-centric with no signal of intent or recent activity to pivot toward systems-level C++ or embedded/IoT domains
